name: Pilot
summary: The ship's driver, combatant, and primary defensive asset. The Pilot controls geometry — positioning, evasion, and engagement. Every crew member's effectiveness depends on where the Pilot puts the ship. The Pilot is the only station that can establish or break [Locked On] through the Dogfight action. In Star Wars, this is the stick-and-rudder role: the person in the cockpit making life-or-death decisions at starfighter speeds.

actions.name: Fly
actions.description: Basic repositioning — move the ship through space. Diceless in open, uncontested space. Rolled only when navigating hazards (asteroid fields, debris), contested by an enemy (blockade, interdictor field), or under time pressure (outrunning a detonation). The Engines Power die determines maximum zone movement: D4–D6 = 1 zone, D8–D10 = 2 zones, D12 = 3 zones. A ship with bigger engines covers more ground. Cannot be used while engaged in a Dogfight — you are locked in until you win and disengage.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Move up to your Engines maximum zones. Smooth transit, no complications.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Move up to your Engines maximum zones. Arrive in an advantageous position — the GM grants a minor narrative benefit (favorable angle, unexpected approach vector, arrived undetected).

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Move up to your Engines maximum zones +1 (exceed normal maximum). Arrive in a dominant position — choose one: arrive behind the target (immediate Dogfight opportunity next round), arrive with concealment (sensor shadow, debris cover), or arrive at a position that cuts off an escape route.

actions.risk: On failure (when rolled), the ship arrives but at a disadvantage — the GM names a complication (overshoot, detected early, clipped an obstacle for Fleeting collision damage).
actions.dogfightConstraint: Cannot be used while engaged in a Dogfight. The ship is locked in the engagement zone until the Pilot wins a Dogfight and chooses to disengage.

actions.name: Dogfight
actions.description: The starship equivalent of melee grapple. Enter a target's zone and engage them in close-quarters maneuvering — a spiraling, high-G contest for positional advantage. Once a Dogfight begins, both ships are locked in the same zone until one Pilot wins and chooses to disengage. The Dogfight winner declares intent: press the attack (establish [Locked On], earn a shot) or break free (clear [Locked On], escape the engagement). A D4 Handling ship is a barge trying to knife-fight. A D12 Handling ship is a blade that cuts angles no other ship can match.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Choose one: establish [Locked On] on the target (you have their tail), OR break [Locked On] on yourself and move 1 zone (you slip out of the engagement).

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Choose one: [Locked On] on the target + an Opening (you or a Gunner may make a reactive attack with pilot-controlled weapons — the split-second firing solution), OR break [Locked On] + move 1 zone + the loser's Gunners are [Weakened] (Tactical) (you escaped clean and left them disoriented).

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Choose one: [Locked On] + Opening + target is [Exposed] (Tactical) (total positional dominance — you own this fight), OR break all locks + move 1 zone + enemy loses contact entirely (cannot close or engage until next round — pursuit is broken).

actions.risk: On failure, the target gets [Locked On] on YOU. You tried to gain the advantage and they reversed you. Universal risk — the aerial grapple flipped.

dogfightRules.engagement: Entering a Dogfight = entering the target's zone and becoming engaged. Both ships are committed to the zone. You cannot Fly or Attack Run while engaged in a Dogfight.
dogfightRules.stuckUntilYouWin: To disengage, you must win a Dogfight and choose the disengage option. There is no separate Disengage action.
dogfightRules.lockedOnRules: [Locked On] is one-directional, single target. You can only have [Locked On] on one target at a time. Any number of ships can have [Locked On] on you.
dogfightRules.multipleAttackers: Multiple ships CAN Dogfight the same target. Each rolls separately against the target's Piloting defense. The target can only actively Dogfight one opponent — the rest roll against their static defense. Every ship that enters the Dogfight is also stuck in the engagement zone.
dogfightRules.zoneDrift: The Dogfight winner may choose to drag the fight to an adjacent zone instead of holding position. Both (or all) engaged ships drift together. This is Zone Drift — the furball moves through space as a side effect of the engagement intensity. Drift into hazardous terrain sets up Terrain Run opportunities.
dogfightRules.actionsWhileDogfighting: While engaged in a Dogfight, the Pilot CAN: Dogfight (press or disengage), Evasive Maneuvers (survive — concedes positional contest), Terrain Run (exploit environment — concedes positional contest). The Pilot CANNOT: Fly (locked in zone), Attack Run (pre-engagement action only). Evade reaction is always available.
dogfightRules.gunnerIndependence: Gunners act independently on their own turns regardless of the Pilot's Dogfight choices. They can fire at targets in range. However, Gunners only receive Dogfight positioning bonuses ([Locked On] benefits, Openings) when the Pilot actively presses the Dogfight — choosing Evasive Maneuvers or Terrain Run instead means the Gunners shoot without those advantages.

actions.name: Attack Run
actions.description: Aggressive positioning — close distance, present firing solutions, and set up the Gunners. The strafing pass, the bombing run approach, the aggressive fly-by. This is a pre-engagement action: the Pilot is putting the ship in position for the crew to shoot, not entering a sustained close-quarters contest. Cannot be used while engaged in a Dogfight — if you are already locked in a turning fight, Attack Run is not available.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Ship closes 1 zone. All Gunners gain [Optimized] on their next attack this round.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Ship closes 1 zone. All Gunners gain [Optimized] and [Empowered] on their next attack this round.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Ship closes 1 zone. All Gunners gain [Optimized] and [Empowered]. The target ship presents an Opening to all Gunners.

actions.risk: On failure, the aggressive approach presents an Opening to the target ship's Gunners. The ship is [Exposed] until the Pilot's next turn.
actions.dogfightConstraint: Cannot be used while engaged in a Dogfight. This is a pre-engagement approach action.

actions.name: Evasive Maneuvers
actions.description: Commit to defensive flying. The ship jukes, rolls, and changes vector to make targeting impossible. CAN be used inside a Dogfight — the Pilot is not contesting for position, just surviving. When used inside a Dogfight, the Pilot concedes the positional contest (the opponent likely maintains or gains [Locked On]) but becomes much harder to hit. When used outside a Dogfight, the Pilot breaks [Locked On] on any target they were pursuing — you cannot evade and chase simultaneously. Does NOT move the ship from its current zone.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Ship gains [Elusive] until start of Pilot's next turn. Incoming attacks have their Control die stepped down.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Ship gains [Elusive] until start of Pilot's next turn. Additionally, one ally station gains [Cover 1] from the ship's positioning.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Ship gains [Elusive] until start of Pilot's next turn. All incoming attacks are stepped down. If NOT in a Dogfight, ship repositions 1 zone as a free movement.

actions.risk: On failure, the ship's vector is predictable — all attacks against the ship until the Pilot's next turn gain [Optimized].
actions.dogfightInteraction: CAN be used inside a Dogfight. The Pilot is choosing survival over advantage — the opponent retains or gains [Locked On], but the Pilot is harder to hit. When used outside a Dogfight, automatically breaks [Locked On] on any target the Pilot was pursuing.

actions.name: Terrain Run
actions.description: Use the environment as a weapon — asteroid fields, debris clouds, atmospheric canyons, station infrastructure. CAN be used inside a Dogfight — especially powerful when Zone Drift has dragged the engagement into hazardous terrain. The Pilot concedes the positional contest but exploits the environment for cover and to punish anyone following. When used outside a Dogfight, breaks [Locked On] on any target — you veered into terrain, not continuing pursuit.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Ship gains [Cover 1] from environmental obstruction until the Pilot's next turn.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Ship gains [Cover 2]. Ships that follow into the terrain take Fleeting collision damage — the path you carved is yours alone.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Ship gains [Cover 2] and concealment. Ships that follow into the terrain take Masterful collision damage — the route you threaded was never meant for anyone else.

actions.risk: On failure, the Pilot clips the terrain — the ship takes Fleeting collision damage and loses [Cover] and concealment.
actions.dogfightInteraction: CAN be used inside a Dogfight. The Pilot concedes the positional contest to exploit the environment. When used outside a Dogfight, automatically breaks [Locked On] on any target the Pilot was pursuing.

reactions.name: Evade
reactions.trigger: Ship is targeted by an attack
reactions.description: The ship's Dodge equivalent. The Pilot rolls Piloting + Handling to evade incoming fire. On success, the Power die result reduces the attacker's Effect Tier. On failure, no mitigation — the hit lands at full effect. On a Control result of 8+, the evasive maneuver generates an Opening for the ship's Gunners. Always available — including inside a Dogfight.
reactions.rule: Success: Power die result reduces attacker's Effect Tier. Failure: no mitigation. Control 8+: generates an Opening — any Gunner with an available Exploit pip may take a reactive attack against the attacker.
reactions.lockedOnInteraction: When the ship is [Locked On] by the attacker, the Evade Power die is [Weakened] — the attacker knows your evasion patterns from the Dogfight engagement.

designNotes: The Pilot's actions split cleanly across two Power dice: Handling (precision — Dogfight, Evasive Maneuvers, Terrain Run, Evade) and Engines (speed/distance — Fly, Attack Run). A ship with great Handling and weak Engines is a knife-fighter that cannot run. A ship with great Engines and weak Handling is fast in a straight line but loses every turning fight.

Dogfight is the starship equivalent of melee grapple. It constrains both ships to the same zone, requires the Pilot's action each round, and only ends when someone wins and chooses to disengage. This creates the same tactical pressure as melee engagement — commitment, risk, and the need for crew coordination to survive a swarm.

Any pilot can fire pilot-controlled weapons (forward cannons, linked guns) using the standard Attack action with Heavy Weapons (Physique) / Fire Control — same as a Gunner. The Ace vocation substitutes Piloting (Reflex) for Heavy Weapons, allowing the Ace to aim with the ship's movement rather than the gun's targeting. This is a discipline swap, not a permission grant.

Pursue/Close and Break Away were merged into the Dogfight action. The winner's tier choices encompass both pressing the attack (establishing [Locked On]) and disengaging (breaking [Locked On] and moving 1 zone). This eliminates the two-action split while preserving all tactical options.

name: Gunner
summary: The ship's offensive firepower. The Gunner operates weapon mounts through the ship's Fire Control system, designates targets, and delivers the damage. Gunners don't control positioning — they execute within the geometry the Pilot established. Multiple Gunners can operate simultaneously if the ship has multiple mounts.

actions.name: Attack
actions.description: Fire a weapon mount at an enemy target. Uses the weapon's chassis damage table to determine damage dealt. Power die comes from the ship's Fire Control system.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Weapon chassis damage (Fleeting tier). Glancing hit — minimal structural impact.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Weapon chassis damage (Masterful tier). Solid hit — meaningful structural damage.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Weapon chassis damage (Legendary tier). Devastating hit — critical structural damage.

actions.risk: On failure, the shot misses. No damage dealt.

actions.name: Called Shot
actions.description: Target a specific system on the enemy ship. If the enemy crew chooses to impair a system to soak damage, they must impair the system you designated.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Weapon chassis damage (Fleeting tier). If the target crew impairs to soak, they must impair the designated system.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Weapon chassis damage (Masterful tier). Designated system must be impaired.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Weapon chassis damage (Legendary tier). Designated system must be impaired. Additionally, the designated system takes one extra step of impairment (Impaired becomes Debilitated, Debilitated becomes Disabled).

actions.risk: On failure, the shot misses. The called targeting was too precise — no damage dealt.

actions.name: Suppressive Fire
actions.description: Saturate a zone with fire to deny movement and create pressure. No direct damage — tactical zone control.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target zone becomes suppressed — any ship that enters or exits the zone this round presents an Opening.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target zone is suppressed. All ships in the zone gain [Suppressed] — they present an Opening if they attempt to move.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target zone is suppressed. All ships in the zone gain [Suppressed] and [Disoriented] on their next action — the volume of fire disrupts targeting and coordination.

actions.risk: On failure, the suppression is ineffective. Ammunition is expended without tactical effect.

reactions.name: Exploit Opening
reactions.trigger: Pilot's Evade reaction generates an Opening (Control 8+)
reactions.description: When the Pilot's Evade generates an Opening, any Gunner with an available Exploit pip may take a reactive attack at -1 Effect Tier (default Exploit rules).
reactions.rule: Standard Exploit — reactive attack at -1 Effect Tier. Requires Exploit pip and a weapon mount that can target the attacker (arc and range must be valid).

gambits.name: Linked Fire
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Fire a second weapon mount in the same arc as part of the same attack action. The second mount resolves at the reduced tier. Both mounts spend ammunition/power.

gambits.name: Ion Pulse
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Instead of dealing hull damage, the attack impairs a system on the target ship. The tier determines impairment severity: Fleeting = Impaired, Masterful = Debilitated, Legendary = Disabled. Only available on mounts with the Ion tag or when the Operator has applied [Jammed] to the target.

gambits.name: Overcharge
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Step up the Fire Control Power die for this attack. After the attack resolves, the fired mount gains [Disabled] — it cannot fire until the Engineer spends an action to reset the weapon.

gambits.name: Concussive Blast
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. The attack detonates in a zone-wide blast. All ships in the target's zone are affected at the reduced tier. Only available on mounts with the Ordnance tag (missiles, torpedoes).

name: Operator
summary: The ship's eyes, ears, and electronic warfare specialist. The Operator reads the universe through the sensor suite — scanning targets, jamming enemy electronics, and deploying countermeasures against guided threats. They determine what the crew knows and what the enemy can see. In Star Wars, this is the com-scan officer: the person monitoring the sensor board, managing the ship's electronic signature, and controlling the flow of information in combat.

actions.name: Scan
actions.description: The starship equivalent of Assess. Point the ship's sensors at a target — a ship, station, planet, asteroid field, debris cloud, anomaly, or any object of interest — and learn about it. Ask the GM questions based on the tier of effect. Works in and out of combat. Not hostile. A D4 sensor suite scanning a far-off contact gets a grainy blip and a rough size estimate. A D12 suite at the same range reads hull markings and counts crew signatures.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Ask the GM 1 question about the target or situation. The GM answers honestly based on what the sensors can reasonably detect at this range and fidelity.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Ask the GM 2 questions. Answers may include details not immediately apparent — power output anomalies, life sign counts, concealed compartments, system damage, internal layout.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Ask the GM 3 questions. Detailed, actionable intelligence. May detect things the target is actively concealing — masked transponder codes, powered-down weapons, a ship running silent in a debris field.

actions.risk: On failure, no useful information gained. Action spent.

actions.name: Jam
actions.description: Electronic attack. Flood the target's sensor frequencies with noise, false returns, and corrupted data to blind their sensors, corrupt their targeting data, and disrupt their communications. This is hostile — an attack action that rolls against the target's Resist defense. A D4 suite is blasting static on every frequency and hoping something sticks. A D12 suite is surgically targeting specific sensor frequencies, cycling through encryption protocols, and burying the target in perfectly crafted false returns.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target gains [Jammed] (Tactical). Sensor data is unreliable — crew judgment compromised by garbage data on every screen.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target gains [Jammed] (Tactical) and their Sensors are [Weakened] (Tactical). Judgment compromised AND sensor output degraded — the instruments are lying and their lies are getting quieter.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target gains [Jammed] (Tactical), Sensors are [Weakened] (Tactical), and the ship is [Exposed] (Tactical). Total electronic collapse — blind, degraded, and vulnerable. Every system that relies on sensor data is compromised.

actions.risk: On failure, no effect. Action spent.

reactions.name: Countermeasures
reactions.trigger: Ship is targeted by guided ordnance (missiles, torpedoes) or an electronic attack (Jam)
reactions.description: Deploy chaff, flares, electronic decoys, or signal spoofing to defend against incoming guided ordnance or electronic warfare attacks. A D4 suite pops a single flare canister and hopes the torpedo chases it. A D12 suite deploys a synchronized cloud of frequency-matched decoys that make every incoming missile think it has already hit something.
reactions.rule: On success, the Power die result reduces the attacker's Effect Tier. On failure, reduce the attacker's Effect Tier by 1 (minimum mitigation floor, identical to Endure). Countermeasure stores are narratively finite — the GM tracks depletion.

name: Engineer
summary: The ship's mechanic and damage control specialist. The Engineer keeps systems running, reinforces defenses, and reroutes power where it is needed most. They do not fire weapons — they make sure everything else works. The Engineer is what keeps a losing fight survivable.

actions.name: Reinforce Shields
actions.description: Proactively boost shield output before the next hit lands. Creates a damage buffer that absorbs incoming fire.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Ship gains [Buffered 1] — absorbs 1 point of incoming hull damage before it lands.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Ship gains [Buffered 2]. The shield reinforcement is clean — no power drain to other systems.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Ship gains [Buffered 3]. Additionally, the shields are so overcharged that the next attack against the ship has its Effect Tier reduced by 1 (shield flare disrupts targeting).

actions.risk: On failure, the shield harmonics destabilize. No buffer gained. Shields are [Disoriented] until the Engineer's next turn.

actions.name: Jury Rig
actions.description: Repair an impaired or disabled system. The Power die is the damaged system's current die level — worse damage means a weaker Power die, making critical repairs harder. All repairs are permanent. Failure is a wasted action with no additional penalty.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Tactical bypass — the system's impairment is ignored for 1 round. The system operates at its pre-damage level until the end of the next round, then reverts. The damage is not repaired — the Engineer is routing around it temporarily.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Permanent repair — the system recovers 1 step of impairment. Debilitated becomes Impaired. Impaired becomes Operational. Disabled becomes Debilitated.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Major repair — recover 2 steps of impairment on one system, OR recover 1 step on two different systems. The kind of repair that should take a dock crew two hours, done under fire in thirty seconds.

actions.risk: On failure, the repair attempt fails. The system remains in its current state. Wasted action — no additional penalty.

actions.name: Reroute Power
actions.description: Trade power between systems — step down one system to step up another. Tactical resource management under fire.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Step down one system's die by 1 size. Step up another system's die by 1 size. Lasts 1 round.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Step down one system's die by 1 size. Step up another system's die by 1 size. Lasts until the end of the fight or until rerouted again.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Step down one system's die by 1 size. Step up the target system's die by 2 sizes. Lasts until the end of the fight. The power routing is so efficient it exceeds the system's rated output.

actions.risk: On failure, the power transfer is unstable. The source system steps down but the target system does not step up. The imbalance persists for 1 round.

name: Co-Pilot
summary: The crew's coordinator and force multiplier. The Co-Pilot does not fly the ship or fire the guns — they make everyone else better at their jobs. Navigation, tactical coordination, and crew support. The Co-Pilot is what separates a crew from a collection of individuals.

actions.name: Coordinate
actions.description: Direct the crew's efforts, share tactical data, and synchronize actions. The starship equivalent of the Command maneuver.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Grant [Optimized] to one crew member's next action this round.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Grant [Optimized] to one crew member and [Empowered] to a different crew member for their next actions this round.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Grant [Optimized] and [Empowered] to all crew members for their next actions this round. The coordination is seamless.

actions.risk: On failure, the coordination is muddled. No buffs granted. One crew member (GM's choice) has their next action [Disoriented] from conflicting instructions.

actions.name: Assist Pilot
actions.description: Directly support the Pilot's actions — managing secondary controls, calling out vectors, or taking the stick for repositioning.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Pilot gains [Optimized] on their next action or Evade reaction this round.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Pilot gains [Optimized] and [Empowered] on their next action or Evade reaction this round.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Pilot gains [Optimized] and [Empowered]. Additionally, the ship may reposition 1 zone as a free movement without requiring a Pilot action.

actions.risk: On failure, the assistance is counterproductive. The Pilot's next action suffers [Disoriented] from conflicting control inputs.

actions.name: Plot Course
actions.description: Calculate navigation solutions — hyperspace jumps, escape routes, or optimal approach vectors.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Rough hyperspace calculation — jump is possible but imprecise. The GM may impose a navigation complication on arrival.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Clean hyperspace calculation — jump is ready when the Pilot calls for it. No complications. Alternatively, identify one optimal escape route that grants +1 Tier to the Pilot's next Dogfight disengage.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Precision jump — ready with tactical advantage. Alternatively, identify a shortcut route granting +2 Tier to the Pilot's next Dogfight disengage and concealment during the approach.

actions.risk: On failure, calculation error. Jump not available this round. If the crew was counting on an escape route, the Pilot's next Dogfight disengage is [Weakened].

gambits.name: Anticipate
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. On a Coordinate action, the buffed crew member's action cannot be [Disoriented] or [Weakened] by enemy abilities until end of round.

gambits.name: Calculated Withdrawal
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. On a Plot Course action at Masterful+, additionally all crew members gain +1 Tier on defensive reactions until end of round. The crew knows the exit is ready — they fight with clarity.
